title: Incremental State Machines, Concurrency, Cancellation, and Ownership · Go Semantics Case: Incremental State Machine
summary: A parser is recursive and side-effectful, multiple goroutines read one connection, cancellation races with response writes, and error paths cannot state which component owns the buffer or close. Planned artifact: Add and verify the incremental state machine increment in explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.
Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Incremental State Machine · Theory Incremental State Machine
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5435 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Incremental State Machine contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Incremental State Machine · Theory Incremental State Machine.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads. For Predict Incremental State Machine · Theory Incremental State Machine, keep Incremental State Machine connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Build Incremental State Machine evidence · Theory Incremental State Machine
